prompt: Plan a full security review covering all 17 modules, prioritized by what's most likely to be exploitable first.
rationale: Enterprise-depth, whole-codebase scope. Tests whether the plan produces a genuinely prioritized, module-by-module structure (e.g. auth and payments before analytics) rather than a flat, unordered list of all 17 modules.

prompt: Plan migrating this entire application to be multi-tenant, with tenant isolation enforced at every module's repository layer.
rationale: Very large architectural change touching all 17 modules' repository files. Tests whether the plan identifies the repeated repository pattern once and describes applying it consistently, rather than writing out near-duplicate steps per module.

prompt: Plan adding structured logging, metrics, and tracing across every module's service layer, building on whatever src/common/logger.ts already provides.
rationale: Large-scope observability rollout that must first ground itself in the existing shared logger utility instead of designing a parallel logging approach from scratch.

prompt: Plan introducing API versioning (v1/v2) across all controllers so future breaking changes don't affect existing clients.
rationale: Broad cross-cutting concern touching every module's controller. Tests whether the plan proposes a consistent, reusable versioning mechanism instead of ad hoc per-module handling.

prompt: Plan a disaster recovery strategy for this application covering data loss, service outage, and a compromised admin account scenario.
rationale: Three distinct disaster scenarios in one request, each requiring different mitigations. Tests whether the plan addresses all three distinctly instead of one generic 'have backups' answer covering all cases.

prompt: Give me the single most exhaustive, maximally detailed plan possible for adding GDPR-compliant data export and deletion across every module that stores user-related data (users, orders, payments, cart, reviews, support-tickets, analytics, audit-log).
rationale: Explicitly asks for maximal exhaustiveness across 8 named modules with real data-privacy implications (e.g. does audit-log data get deleted or retained for compliance). Tests depth/enterprise-scale plan generation without losing coherence or omitting the named modules.
